negative_variants.description: Vector Replication Mask: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Vector Replication Mask: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Vector Replication Mask: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Vector Replication Mask: keeps the main datapath plausible while reporting the wrong metric value.
Vector Replication Mask: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
